claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 3 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

Actually, I should mention - I have three children. Alex is 3 years old, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10. Do you need to go through each of them separately, or how does this work?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great that you've told me about all three children! Since Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10) are all **under 16 years old**, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit, which simplifies things.

I don't need to go through each child separately for the age check, but I will need to ask you some questions that apply to your situation as the claimant, and a few questions about the children. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Since all three of your children are under 16, they wouldn't be in full-time work (24+ hours per week), so I can move forward on that basis.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) receiving benefits in their own right, such as:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

This would be quite unusual for children under 16, but I need to check. Are all three children **not** receiving these types of benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are receiving any benefits in their own names. They're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense - they're young children, so that's not a concern at all!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Given that Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10, they're all too young to be in any apprenticeship programmes, so we can move on.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check about your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following residency requirements?

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common situation)
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You are a **Crown servant** living abroad

The most common is simply that you **normally live in the UK**. Does that apply to you, or does one of the other situations fit better?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK. We're based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's straightforward then - you meet the residency requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about your responsibility for the children:**

Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie? This can be met if:

1. **You live with the children** (most common), OR
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** (e.g., paying for food, clothes, pocket money, housing), OR
3. **You pay maintenance** under a court order or agreement

Do all three children live with you, or are you responsible for them in another way?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex and Charlie live at home with me. Blake doesn't live with me though - he lives with his mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for clarifying that. So for Alex and Charlie, you clearly meet the responsibility requirement as they live with you.

For Blake, since he doesn't live with you, I need to check if you're responsible for him in another way.

**Regarding Blake:**

Do you contribute to Blake's upkeep, such as:
- Paying for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents
- Making maintenance payments (under a court order or agreement)

The contribution would need to be at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment (or equivalent in kind). Do you contribute to Blake's care in any of these ways?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing anything financially towards Blake's care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for being upfront about that. Unfortunately, this means you wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake, since you're not currently living with him or contributing to his upkeep.

However, you may still be eligible for Alex and Charlie since they live with you. Let me continue the assessment for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie?

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A family member they sometimes stay with

If someone else is claiming, has there been an agreement about who should receive the benefit? Or is there any dispute about this?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Charlie. They both live with me full-time, so I'm the one looking to claim for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's straightforward then.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about where the children are living:**

Are Alex and Charlie currently in any of these situations?

- **In hospital** (for an extended stay)
- **In local authority care**
- **Being fostered**
- **Living normally at home with you** (the usual situation)

I assume they're both living normally at home with you in Manchester - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Well, Alex and Charlie are living normally at home with me, yes. 

But actually, I should mention - Blake has been in hospital for quite a while now. He's been there for a few months.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for mentioning that about Blake. I'm sorry to hear he's been in hospital for a while - that must be difficult.

However, since you mentioned earlier that you don't live with Blake and aren't contributing to his upkeep, you wouldn't meet the responsibility requirement for claiming Child Benefit for him regardless of his hospital stay. Blake's mum (who he lives with) would be the one who might be able to claim for him.

So let's continue with the assessment for Alex and Charlie, who are living normally at home with you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is the local council **not** paying towards Alex's or Charlie's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, are you supporting them without council financial assistance for their housing or upkeep? (This is the case for most families.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - I'm supporting them myself. The council isn't paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That completes the assessment for Alex and Charlie.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete! 🎉

Here's a summary of your Child Benefit eligibility:

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| **Alex** | 3 | ✅ **Yes** |
| **Blake** | 8 | ❌ **No** |
| **Charlie** | 10 | ✅ **Yes** |

---

### **Alex & Charlie - ELIGIBLE** ✅
Both children meet all the eligibility requirements:
- Under 16 years old
- Living with you in the UK
- You are responsible for them
- No one else is claiming for them
- The council isn't paying for their accommodation

### **Blake - NOT ELIGIBLE (for you to claim)** ❌
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake because:
- He doesn't live with you
- You're not currently contributing to his upkeep

**However**, Blake's mum may be able to claim Child Benefit for him since he lives with her.

---

### **Next Steps to Claim for Alex and Charlie:**

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Charlie
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit Charge
5. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

👉 **Apply here:** [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, no other claimant, living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) does not meet the eligibility criteria for you to claim. Although Blake is under 16 and would otherwise qualify based on age, you do not meet the 'responsibility for child' requirement. Blake does not live with you (he lives with his mum), and you are not currently contributing financially to his upkeep. To claim Child Benefit, you must either live with the child OR contribute to their upkeep at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount.

Charlie (age 10) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, no other claimant, living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. Alex and Charlie both live with you full-time in Manchester, making you responsible for them, and they meet all other eligibility requirements. However, you cannot claim for Blake as he lives with his mum and you are not contributing to his upkeep - Blake's mum may be able to claim Child Benefit for him instead.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 3) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, no other claimant, living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) does not meet the eligibility criteria for you to claim. Although Blake is under 16 and would otherwise qualify based on age, you do not meet the 'responsibility for child' requirement. Blake does not live with you (he lives with his mum), and you are not currently contributing financially to his upkeep. To claim Child Benefit, you must either live with the child OR contribute to their upkeep at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount.

Charlie (age 10) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, no other claimant, living normally at home, and the council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for two of your three children. Alex and Charlie both live with you full-time in Manchester, making you responsible for them, and they meet all other eligibility requirements. However, you cannot claim for Blake as he lives with his mum and you are not contributing to his upkeep - Blake's mum may be able to claim Child Benefit for him instead.
